sections.paragraph:
Ἔραστος, ου, ὁ Erastus (the name is found in lit. and ins; s. SIG 838, 6).

① a Christian at Corinth, designated as οἰκονόμος τῆς πόλεως city treasurer Ro 16:23. A Lat. ins fr. Corinth, published in the lit. quoted below, mentions an official named Erastus.

② a companion of Paul Ac 19:22; 2 Ti 4:20.—FdeWaele, Erastus: Mededeelingen van het Nederlandsch Hist. Inst. te Rome 9, 1929, 40–48; HCadbury, E. of Cor.: JBL 50, ’31, 42–58; WMiller, Who was E.?: BiblSacra 88, ’31, 342–46; Hemer, Acts 235, 260 n. 32; Boffo, Iscrizioni no. 43; BHHW I 422.—LGPN I. M-M.

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

Lat. Lat. = Latin

  1. fr. = from

Hist.

Hist. (after a personal name)=historicus, historian

hist. = historian, historical

JBL JBL = Journal of Biblical Literature—List 6

BiblSacra BiblSacra = Bibliotheca Sacra, A Theological Quarterly—List 6

Hemer, Acts Hemer, Acts = CH., The Book of Acts in the Setting of Hellenistic History—List 6

Boffo, Iscrizioni Boffo, Iscrizioni = LB., Iscrizioni greche e latine—List 3

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

LGPN LGPN = A Lexicon of Greek Personal Names—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 388
